traps: amazonSageMaker is a subServiceSelector parent. Call addService('sageMakerStudioNotebooks', ...), addService('sageMakerRealTimeInference', ...), or any other child code; EstimateBuilder merges sibling children into a single amazonSageMaker parent envelope. Do NOT call addService('amazonSageMaker', ...) directly — that key has no fields of its own.

Each child has its OWN templateId and field set — do not assume parent-level fields. Studio Notebooks uses templateId 'sagemakerStudioNotebook'; Real-Time Inference uses 'realtimeInferenceInstances'. EstimateBuilder discovers each child's templateId from its PCT.

Instance type is a columnFormIPM, NOT a top-level field. Send the WRAPPED shape: { columnFormIPM: { value: [{ 'Instance Name': { value: '<ml.*>' } }] } } — NOT the dotted-path 'columnFormIPM[0].Instance Name' shorthand. validateConfigKeys rejects unknown top-level keys. The single column's selectorId is the literal string 'Instance Name' (autoSuggest cell). Values must be SageMaker-prefixed instance names (ml.t3.medium, ml.m5.large, ml.g5.xlarge, etc.) drawn from get_service_fields(<child>).columnFormIPM.selectorValues['Instance Name']. SageMaker columnFormIPMs have NO 'Number of Nodes', utilization, or TermType columns — instance count is governed by the scalar fields (instancesPerEndPoint for inference, NotebooksPerMonth × DataScientistsPerMonth for Studio).

sageMakerRealTimeInference exposes THREE columnFormIPM fields: 'columnFormIPM' (primary hosting instances), 'columnFormIPM_3' (Elastic Inference accelerators, gated by EIAInstancesPerm=Yes), and 'columnFormIPM2' (Model Monitor instances, gated by modelMonitorJobsPerMonth>0). Send only 'columnFormIPM' for a minimal estimate; the other two stay defaulted unless you explicitly enable EI or Model Monitor.

Real-Time Inference's SM_storageAmount is gated by SM_storageType — sending storage size alone won't surface unless storage type is also set. The dropdown has only one option ('General Purpose' = gp2). Storage adds a small fixed charge per endpoint instance.

required.hint: Number of data scientists using Studio per month
Number of Studio Notebook instances per data scientist
Hours per day each notebook runs
Days per month each notebook runs
Hosting instance type for the Studio notebook (autoSuggest, key 'Instance Name'). Cheapest reasonable: ml.t3.medium.
Total number of distinct models deployed
Number of models hosted per endpoint
Number of hosting instances backing each endpoint
Hours per day each endpoint runs (24 = always-on)
Days per month each endpoint runs (30 = always-on)
Hosting instance type for the inference endpoint (autoSuggest, key 'Instance Name'). Cheapest reasonable: ml.m5.large.
